comment_text.sn7.16:1.2: Paccanīkasāta, “Pleased by contradiction”. Even though the construction “named” is used, it is clearly an epithet rather than a proper name.

root_text.sn7.16:1.2: Tena kho pana samayena paccanīkasāto nāma brāhmaṇo sāvatthiyaṁ paṭivasati.
root_text.sn7.16:1.5: Yaṁ yadeva samaṇo gotamo bhāsissati taṁ tadevassāhaṁ paccanīkāssan”ti.
root_text.sn7.16:1.6: Tena kho pana samayena bhagavā abbhokāse caṅkamati.
root_text.sn7.16:1.7: Atha kho paccanīkasāto brāhmaṇo yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ caṅkamantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn7.16:4.1: Evaṁ vutte, paccanīkasāto brāhmaṇo bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn7.16:4.3: upāsakaṁ maṁ bhavaṁ gotamo dhāretu ajjatagge pāṇupetaṁ saraṇaṁ gatan”ti.

translation_text.sn7.16:1.2: Now at that time a brahmin named Contraphile, who loved contradiction, was residing in Sāvatthī.
translation_text.sn7.16:1.4: “Why don’t I go to the ascetic Gotama and
translation_text.sn7.16:1.6: At that time the Buddha was walking mindfully in the open air.
translation_text.sn7.16:1.7: Then the brahmin Contraphile went up to the Buddha, and said to him as he was walking,
translation_text.sn7.16:3.3: and you’ve got rid of resentment,
translation_text.sn7.16:4.1: When he had spoken, the brahmin Contraphile said to the Buddha,
translation_text.sn7.16:4.3: From this day forth, may the worthy Gotama remember me as a lay follower who has gone for refuge for life.”

variant_text.sn7.16:1.5: tadevassāhaṁ → tadeva sāhaṁ (mr) | paccanīkāssan”ti → paccanikāssanti (sya-all); paccanīkassanti (pts1ed); paccanīkasātanti (mr)
